Class TileView

java.lang.Object
javafx.scene.Node
javafx.scene.Parent
javafx.scene.Group
view.TileView
All Implemented Interfaces:
javafx.css.Styleable, javafx.event.EventTarget
public class TileView extends javafx.scene.Group
Represents the visual component of a single tile on the game board within the JavaFX scene. This class manages the rendering of the tile's base terrain texture, interactive color overlays (for game states like selected, reachable, or attackable), and dynamic environmental sprites (such as forests or fire). It utilizes an optimized dual-polygon rendering technique to minimize expensive JavaFX scene graph redraws during gameplay.

    resetFlags()
    Clears all temporary visual state flags (selected, reachable, hovered, etc.) simultaneously.

    Sets whether the tile can currently be targeted for an attack and triggers a visual style update.
    void

    Sets whether the tile is currently marked as a danger zone and triggers a visual style update.

    Sets whether the tile is a valid zone for unit deployment and triggers a visual style update.

    Sets whether the tile is currently within an active attack impact zone and triggers a visual style update.

    Sets whether the tile is currently reachable for movement and triggers a visual style update.

    Sets the tile's selected state and triggers a visual style update.

    Updates the physical dimensions, screen coordinates, and z-ordering of the tile.
    void

    Calculates and applies the final visual appearance of the tile based on all active state flags.

    Methods inherited from interface javafx.css.Styleable

    getStyleableNode

  • Constructor Details

    • TileView

      public TileView(Tile logicTile)
      Constructs a new TileView bound to the provided logical tile instance. Initializes the dual-polygon structural layers, sets up default stroke styling, and registers mouse event listeners (hover and click) to pass interactions back to the GameManager.
      Parameters:
      logicTile - The logical Tile object this view accurately represents.

  • Method Details

    • setSelected

      public void setSelected(boolean selected)
      Sets the tile's selected state and triggers a visual style update.
      Parameters:
      selected - true if the tile is selected; false otherwise.

    • setReachable

      public void setReachable(boolean reachable)
      Sets whether the tile is currently reachable for movement and triggers a visual style update.
      Parameters:
      reachable - true if the tile can be moved to; false otherwise.

    • setAttackable

      public void setAttackable(boolean attackable)
      Sets whether the tile can currently be targeted for an attack and triggers a visual style update.
      Parameters:
      attackable - true if the tile is within attack range; false otherwise.

    • setImpact

      public void setImpact(boolean impacted)
      Sets whether the tile is currently within an active attack impact zone and triggers a visual style update.
      Parameters:
      impacted - true if the tile is about to be impacted; false otherwise.

    • setDanger

      public void setDanger(boolean danger)
      Sets whether the tile is currently marked as a danger zone and triggers a visual style update.
      Parameters:
      danger - true if the tile is hazardous; false otherwise.

    • setDeployable

      public void setDeployable(boolean deployable)
      Sets whether the tile is a valid zone for unit deployment and triggers a visual style update.
      Parameters:
      deployable - true if a unit can be deployed here; false otherwise.

    • resetFlags

      public void resetFlags()
      Clears all temporary visual state flags (selected, reachable, hovered, etc.) simultaneously. Note: This method does *not* automatically call updateStyle(); the caller must invoke it manually if a visual redraw is required immediately.

    • update

      public void update(double screenX, double screenY, double width, double height)
      Updates the physical dimensions, screen coordinates, and z-ordering of the tile. This method is highly optimized: it translates the X/Y coordinates every frame, but only reconstructs the computationally expensive polygon geometry if the camera zoom (width/height) has actually changed.
      Parameters:
      screenX - The projected absolute X coordinate on the screen.
      screenY - The projected absolute Y coordinate on the screen.
      width - The current isometric width of the tile (determined by camera zoom).
      height - The current isometric height of the tile (determined by camera zoom).

    • updateStyle

      public void updateStyle()
      Calculates and applies the final visual appearance of the tile based on all active state flags. This centralized method manages sprite visibility (fire/forests), calculates blended overlay colors to accurately represent multiple states simultaneously (e.g., being both reachable and hovered), and applies dynamic glow/shadow effects to both the tile surface and its attached sprites.
